note: Nāgajjuna (Nāgārjuna), Mahāyāna ācariya, paired with Asvaghosa
channāgāriko = Channāgārika school name (chaṇṇa+agāra, 'six-room'); not the nāga morpheme
punāgamiṃsu = puna+āgamiṃsu 'came back'; sandhi false friend
Nāgajjuna/Nāgārjuna the Mahāyāna teacher; nāgārajunakoṇḍa place named after him

claim: rebirth from the niraya/nāga-realm (nirayanāgayoni) conditions a dosa-temperament
note: nāgayoni listed as a destination/origin of rebirth
Dāṭhānāga thera who requested the Dīgha commentary
Asvaghosa-Nāgajjuna-Vasubandhu, Mahāyāna teachers; nānāgaṇā=nānā+gaṇā, dāṭhādināgena=Dāṭhā-nāga thera
anāgārehi = an+agāra 'homeless'; sandhi false friend
nāganāsūrū 'elephant-trunk thighs', nāgassa nāsā viya ūrū; nāginī/nāgī forms in grammar paradigm
nāge in list gandhabbe-rakkhase-nāge-kimpurise-mānuse; being among beings
nāgamo = na+āgamo, grammatical augment; tattha nāgamo 'there is no augment'
ayaṃ nāgo, alaṃ dukkhāya kāyaci (Jataka) 'this elephant'; pronoun grammar example
nāgassa duvesu dantesu 'two tusks of the elephant'; nāgamo = augment (nonlexical) but primary nāga sense here is elephant via dantesu
namo karohi nāgassa 'pay homage to the nāga'; case-grammar example, referent not fixed
rañño nāgaṃ dhārayāma 'we owe/keep the king's elephant'; debt/royal-elephant context
bhoginaṃ glossed nāgaṃ; 'bhogin' (coiled one) = serpent, nāhaṃ bhāyāmi bhoginaṃ
buddhanāgo in kammadhāraya list munisīho/munipuṅgavo/buddhanāgo/buddhādicco; nāga as honorific of the Buddha
nāgagati/nāgavikkamo 'elephant's gait/stride' as similes; nāgassa viya gati
nāgavatā/sīhavatā/ājaññavatā suffix example; nāga-/sīha-/ājañña- pattern, referent not fixed (elephant or serpent or noble)
kānubandhanāgamesu/nāgame = (n)āgama grammatical augment
dakkhemu te nāga nivesanāni 'we shall see your, O nāga, dwellings'; Jataka uraga context (muñcemu naṃ uragaṃ)
kānubandhanāgamesu = augment grammar; bare 'nāga' here is part of sutta-rule string nāgamesu
kānubandhanāgamesū = (n)āgama grammatical augment rule
nāgamo = na+āgamo 'no augment'; grammar
nāgame/nāgamo = (n)āgama augment; nāgamekhipanako = nāgame+khipanako
nāgo dantesu haññate 'the elephant is killed for its tusks'; classic etymological simile of elephant

claim: an iddhi-adept can transform into nāga-form (nāgavaṇṇaṃ) or garuḷa-form at will
note: shapeshifting iddhi context; nāgavaṇṇaṃ vā garuḷavaṇṇaṃ vā katvā

claim: nāgas are subjects of Virūpakkha (one of the four great kings); nāgas/yakkhas largely of evil disposition
note: Virūpakkho nāgānaṃ (rājā); nāgā in cosmological account; mahāhatthināgassa elsewhere = elephant-simile
cetanāgahaṇaṃ = cetanā+gahaṇaṃ 'taking of volition'; sandhi false friend

claim: vikubbana-iddhi: adept abandons own form and manifests as a youth, a nāga, etc.
note: kumāravaṇṇanāgavaṇṇādikaṃ katvā; shapeshift into nāga-appearance
racanāgāthāyaṃ = racana+gāthāyaṃ 'in the composition-verse'; sandhi false friend

claim: bhumma-deva/yakkha births are reckoned among the nāgas (nāgesu saṅgayhanti)
note: nāgātveva vuccanti, nāgesu saṅgayhanti; classification of earth-deities under nāga; nāgasupaṇṇādīnaṃ

claim: nāga-kings (Mahākāḷa, Vāsuki) and nāga-hosts attended/honored the Buddha at the Bodhi/awakening scene
note: Namakkāraṭīkā mixed: nāgarājā/Mahākāḷanāga/Vāsukī nāgarājā = serpent-kings present; also nāgasenattherena (person) & anāgāriya (nonlexical) & mahānāga-of-teacher (epithet) in token list, but dominant nāga sense in window = serpent-king homage

claim: the Buddha possesses the strength of a koṭi-thousand nāgas (koṭisahassanāgabaliko); nāgindo as chief of nāgas
note: anubyañjana praise; nāga-strength simile asserting nāga power; nāgusabho/nāgindena
pālileyyaka vane pahāya nāge 'leaving the elephant(s) in Pālileyyaka forest'; the Pālileyya elephant episode

cūḷodarādikenāge = ...kena+nāge? actually punāgamesi=puna+āgamesi 'returned' (nonlexical); cūḷodarādikenāge ambiguous but the live token punāgamesituragaṃ = puna+āgamesi; sandhi false friend dominates
chaddantanāgarājāva 'like the Chaddanta nāga-king'; simile of the Buddha's power (here Chaddanta as nāga-king title in praise verse)
devā ca nāgā asurā ca yakkhā; nāgas in standard list of beings attending the birth

claim: the nāga-realm/dwellings (nāgānaṃ bhavanesu) have their characteristic flowers (kumuda, uppala)
note: nāgānaṃ bhavanesu = nāga abodes (serpent); nāgapunnāgaketakā elsewhere in window = tree (flower list), but the live claim-bearing token is nāgānaṃ bhavanesu

claim: Vāsuki the nāga-king served/protected the Buddha and was held by the Dhamma-teaching
note: Pajjamadhu praise: vāsuki nāgarājā; serpent-king devotion
dhammanāgo/mahānāgo/naranāgo/nāgavaro of the Buddha in a litany of honorifics; nāga as title of the Buddha; anāgārehi=nonlexical, nunnāgu obscure
Buddhanāgo nāmācariyo who wrote the Vinayatthamañjūsā ṭīkā
buddhanāgācariyo in a list of teacher-names
buddhanāgācariyena, the ācariya who composed the ṭīkā
khīṇāsavamahānāgehi 'by the great-nāga arahants (canker-destroyed)'; mahānāga as honorific of arahants who recited the Dhamma

channāgārikonāma = Channāgārika school (chaṇṇa+agāra); susunāga(putto/rañño) = King Susunāga (person but the live tokens here are school-name + Susunāga king); dominant: school-name nonlexical + king name
succession of elders: nāgo, cūḷanāgo, mahānāgo, coranāgo as personal names of theras; nāgarā=citizens incidentally but live -nāga tokens are elder names

claim: a nāgī (she-nāga) mated with a vijjādhara, laid two eggs from which two humans were born
note: nāgiyā vijjādharo...sā nāgī; nāga taking part in human-generating union; mahānāgavaṃso also = lineage name
Nāgasena thera (Milinda), nāgasenattherassa/nāgasenattheraṃ; the monk Nāgasena
jaṭilo sakko nāgo garuḷo... 'a jaṭila, Sakka, a nāga, a garuḷa' as founders of a city; could be serpent-being but listed among personified city-founders; underdetermined

claim: the nāga Erāpatha seized relics from the water to enshrine them; nāga-king relic-custody narrative
note: mixed window: erāpatho nāma nāgo gahetvā (serpent), dāṭhānāga thera/Nāgita/nāgarika incidental; chaddantanāgarāja-uppatti = serpent origin story; dominant nāga sense serpent

claim: the nāga-king Aravāḷa caused destructive hail-storms over the Kasmīra-Gandhāra crops until subdued
note: aravāḷo nāma nāgarājā...karakavassaṃ; weather-power; locals make nāga-images to worship them; mahānāga (of teacher)=epithet incidental
Mahānāgavanuyyāne, the Mahānāga pleasure-grove (place name)

claim: the nāga-kings Mahodara and Cūḷodara, uncle and nephew, warred over a jewelled throne; the Buddha visited the nāga-realm (Nāgadīpa) to reconcile them
note: Mahodara/Cūḷodara nāgas, samudde nāgabhavane, nāgadīpa; Buddha teaches the nāgas; rich serpent narrative
nāgamā = na+āgamā 'did not come'; tehi nāgamā 'he did not come with them'
Nāgadāsaka the parricide king (nāgadāsako); susunāgoti = King Susunāga; nāgarā=citizens incidental, live -nāga tokens are king names
channāgārā = Channāgārika school (chaṇṇa+agāra); list of schism schools
nāgalatādantakaṭṭhaṃ 'nāga-creeper tooth-stick'; nāgalatā = betel/creeper (plant); nāgānāgāvimānato obscure compound

claim: King Asoka summoned the mighty nāga-king Mahākāḷa (who had seen four Buddhas) by a chain-binding spell to honor him
note: mahākāḷaṃ nāgarājaṃ mahiddhikaṃ, soṇḍasaṅkhalibandhanaṃ; nāgalatā (creeper)=tree token incidental; dominant serpent-king
sanāgami = saha+nāgami? actually sa+anāgami / 'sa nāgami' = 'so na+āgami' he did not come; nāgena ambiguous but window is about the thera being summoned and not coming; sandhi false friend dominates

claim: nāgas brought (nāgāhaṭaṃ) collyrium and special substances as royal-consecration gifts
note: nāgāhaṭaṃ añjanaṃ 'collyrium brought by nāgas'; nāgas supplying marvellous gifts implies their realm/powers

claim: the nāga-king Aravāḷa, enraged, caused storms, lightning and falling mountain-peaks until Majjhantika subdued him
note: aravālo nāgarājā vassaṃ karakasaññitaṃ; weather-power and anger of serpent-king
Mahānāga the sub-king (kaniṭṭhassa oparājassa mahānāgassa); nāgarā=citizens incidental, live mahānāga token = prince's name

mixed Sīhaḷa chronicle: mahānāgassa (prince), nāgamālake/nāgamāḷaka (place-name 'Nāga-court'), nāgenā (a nāga who invited the Buddha at Kalyāṇī), nāgānaṃ damanaṃ (taming of nāgas); window is dominated by place-name + prince but does contain a serpent reference; judgement: mahānāgassa + nāgamālaka (citizen/person/place) outweigh; coding person for the live mahānāga proper name
nāgacatukkamhi 'at the Nāga-square/crossroads' (place name) where they bathed
Mahānāgavanuyyānaṃ, the Mahānāga pleasure-grove (place name); nāgarā=citizens, nāgo incidental

claim: nāgas (with devas and humans) honored the great Bodhi tree; the taming of nāgas (nāgadamana) and a nāga-kingdom (nāgarajja) are referenced
note: nāgayakkhānaṃ, devānāgānarāpi, nāgadamanaṃ, nāgarajjena; nāgas as worshipping/tamed beings
nāgadīpe (Nāgadīpa, place named for nāgas); vinayādijānāgamaṃ = ...jāna+āgamaṃ (nonlexical) but live nāgadīpe = serpent-island place; passing geographic mention
nāgamahāvihāraṃ = Nāgamahāvihāra named after the king (sakanāmakaṃ 'after his own name'); mahānāgo = the king's name
dasanāgabalo 'having the strength of ten elephants' epithet of Nandimitta the warrior; mahānāgassa = a person; the live tokens describe a human's strength (idiom) and a proper name
nāgamuṃ = na+agamuṃ 'they did not go (to war)'; taṃ yuddhaṃ tena nāgamuṃ
nāgarājā Kaṇḍula, the king's war-elephant (gajo), retreating in battle; nāgo of the elephant; nānāgandhasamāyute=nānā+gandha (nonlexical), senāgā=senā+agā (nonlexical)
kāḷanāgo sanāṭiko in a list with Pañcasikha, Sakka, Māra at the Bodhi-shrine scene; Kāḷanāga likely a serpent-king attendant but could be a personified actor; underdetermined from window

claim: the nāga-king Kāḷa(nāga) guarded the relics in the Mañjerika nāga-realm; the thera entered the nāga-world and recovered them by iddhi
note: kāḷanāgassa, mañjerikanāgabhavanaṃ, nāgalokaṃ, nāgaranāriyo (nāga-city women), nāgarājā, nāgindo; full serpent relic-custody narrative
Khallāṭanāga the king (khallāṭanāganāmako, khallāṭanāgarājānaṃ); mahānāga / mahāvihāranāgamuṃ as king/place names
Coranāga the king (coranāgoti vissuto, coranāgo akārayi); Mahādāṭhikamahānāga, Maṇināga as royal names
Iḷanāga and Mahallanāga, kings (iḷanāgoti, mahallanāgabhūpati 'King Mahallanāga'); nāgadīpe/nāgamahāvihāra/tobbalanāgapabbata = place names after them
Cūḷanāga, Kuḍḍanāga, Sirināga, Bhayanāga, Mūkanāga = succession of kings/senāpati; nāgadīpe place name; all -nāga royal personal names
the king mounts a great tusker (hatthikkhandha) and finds a sick mahānāga (elephant with belly-disease) at an anthill; mahānāga as elephant; pun 'mahānāgo mahānāgamanāgavā'

claim: a nāga-king (dhātunāga) was directed to guard the relic; nāga set to protect the relic-chamber
note: dhātunāgaṃ samappayi, nāgarājā, suvā nāgaṃ samādisi 'ordered the nāga'; serpent relic-guardian (mahānāge here could be elephant but nāgarājā fixes serpent)

claim: a nāga-king (nāginda/mahānāga) is summoned from the ocean by mantra and compelled to 'touch' (confer kingship on) a man
note: mantonānesi nāgindaṃ, mahānāgaṃ phusāhi at gokaṇṇa ocean; an arahant thera also named Mahānāga (person) appears, but the operative nāga act = serpent-king coerced by spell
Mahānāga the king (mahānāganarindassa, mahānāgassa); kapālanāgaṃ/nāgasoṇḍiṃ place features, nāgadīpa; anāgamma=an+āgamma (nonlexical); dominant -nāga = royal name Mahānāga

Sirināga (sirināgavho), Nāgasāla place/āvāsa; rañño nāgo here = king's war-elephant but live dominant proper name is Sirināga + Nāgasāla monastery; balenāgā=balena+agā (nonlexical). Mixed; coding person for Sirināga king-name
Nāgavihāraka / Nāgasālā = monastery/place names receiving village-grants
nāgasālanivāsinaṃ '(thera) residing at Nāgasāla' (place name); Dāṭhāsiva thera of Nāgasāla
mahānāgaṃ ghoraṃ mārakarūpamaṃ, a fierce war-elephant (saṅgamāvacaraṃ gajaṃ, kuñjaraṃ); mahānāga = battle-elephant
Nāgavaḍḍhana, a personal name granted villages (nāgavaḍḍhananāmassa bhogagāme); proper name
Nāgasāla = the Nāgasāla cloister/place (pariveṇaṃ nāgasālaṃ); nāgarā=citizens incidental
nāgadīpaṃ (Nāgadīpa, place named for nāgas); dāṭhānāgā/dāṭhā-nāga thera + bandhanāgāre=bandhana+agāre (prison, nonlexical); dominant geographic nāga = serpent-island; passing mention
Mahānāgahula, a town/city name (mahānāgahulavhaye 'named Mahānāgahula'); kamenāgamma=kamena+āgamma (nonlexical)
Mahānāgahula, town name (mahānāgahulaṃ puraṃ 'the city Mahānāgahula')
Mahānāgahula town name (mahānāgahulavhaye); candanāgarukappuraṃ = candana+agaru+kappura 'sandal, aloeswood, camphor' (nonlexical compound)
nānāguṇā = nānā+guṇā 'various qualities'; sandhi false friend
jināgamesu = jina+āgamesu 'in the Jina's scriptures'; sandhi false friend
kānubandhanāgamesu = (n)āgama grammatical augment rule (Kaccayana sutta heading)
sāmattiyenāgamova = sāmatthiyena+āgamo+va; āgama = grammatical augment; niggahītamāgamo context
nāgasupaṇṇaṃ in niccaverī (natural-enemy) dvanda list ahi-nakula, nāga-supaṇṇa; nāga vs garuḷa enmity = serpent; nāginī/nāgī grammar forms
kānubandhanāgamato/kānubandhanāgamesu/nāgame = (n)āgama grammatical augment

nāgarikabhāvasaṅkhātassa 'of what is called the urbane/citified state' (kilesa-siṅga metaphor); nāgarika = urban/citizen; kānubandhanāgamesū = augment (nonlexical) but live distinct token nāgarika = citizen
anāgārehi = an+agāra 'homeless'; sandhi (samannāgato also present)
nāgiyo as grammar declension example (i-stem feminine), nāgī she-nāga; lexical nāga form, default serpent
devā asurā nāgā gandhabbā manussā; nāgas in standard being-list used as grammar example
bhāyāmi ghoravisassa nāgassa 'I fear the fierce-venomed nāga'; venomous serpent (āsīvisa context)
mallināgarukkha / vallināgarukkha / nāgarukkho = nāga-tree (ironwood); flowering trees kissed by bees; mahānāgo incidental; dominant tokens = nāga-tree
nāgaṃ raṭṭhassa pūjitaṃ 'the elephant honored by the kingdom'; grammar example of singular-for-plural, royal elephant
munināgena 'by the muni-nāga' = the Buddha; muninā munināgena in Saddanīti verse; nāga as honorific of the Buddha
nāgasena (Nāgasena, declension paradigm example); also aṭṭha nāgāvāsasatāni 'eight hundred nāga-abodes' (serpent) and dāṭhānāga; mixed grammar window; live dominant proper name Nāgasena = person
sā maddī nāgamāruhi (nāgaṃ āruhi) 'Maddī mounted the elephant' (Vessantara); nātibaddhaṃva kuñjaraṃ confirms elephant; pronoun-grammar example
nayavasenāgahetabbattā = naya-vasena+a-gahetabba-ttā 'because not to be taken by way of method'; sandhi false friend
nāganāsūrū 'elephant-trunk thighs' in ū-stem feminine paradigm; nāgī form also; trunk = elephant

devinda-nāginda-narinda-vinda in praise of the Buddha (muninda); nāginda 'lord of nāgas' used in honorific cluster addressed to the Buddha; nāgamāruyha = nāgaṃ āruyha incidental. Coding epithet (the verse glorifies the Buddha as chief among nāgindas etc.)
Nāgadīpa (nāgadīpo, nāgadīpādīnaṃ) discussed as the place-name built on nāga-; nāgasaddādinā 'by the word nāga etc.'; place named for serpents
nāgarājā, ke gandhabbe ca rakkhase nāge; nāga in standard being-list and nāga-king as grammar examples
yathā āraññakaṃ nāgaṃ dantiṃ anveti hatthinī 'as the cow-elephant follows the forest tusker'; dantiṃ/hatthinī fix elephant
hatthi nāgo, hatthī ca kuñjaro nāgo, nāgaṃ dantiṃ anveti hatthinī; nāga = synonym of elephant (hatthi/kuñjara/dantī)

nāgapetavatthuaṭṭhakathāyaṃ = a text-title ('Nāga-petavatthu commentary') cited only as the source for the word suvaggita; the nāga morpheme is a title artefact, not operative in the window's argument
brāhmaṇo nāgaṃ maṇiṃ yācati 'the brahmin begs a jewel from the nāga'; nāga possessing a maṇi (jewel) = serpent-being; verb-grammar example
explicit synonym list: hatthī nāgo gajo dantī kuñjaro vāraṇo karī...; nāga = elephant
nāgadhāmo in a list of synonyms for rasmi/ray (rasmi ābhā pabhā... nāgadhāmo ca āloko); 'nāgadhāma' = a name for sunbeam/ray, not the nāga-being; coding nonlexical (different lexeme, ray-word)

claim: a nāga is asked what vow/brahmacariya it kept to gain its mansion and powers (iddhi-juti-bala); nāga keeping observances
note: idañca te nāga mahāvimānaṃ; addressing a nāga about its vata/uposatha and resulting vimāna (Vimanavatthu/Petavatthu-type), serpent who kept vows
nāgāri 'enemy of nāgas' = garuḷa/supaṇṇa (garuḷo... nāgāri); nāga referent is the serpent (as the garuḷa's prey)

mixed dhātu examples: yo me saṃse mahānāgaṃ (could be elephant/serpent), nāgapāso 'nāga-noose', nāgarājā, nāganāsūrū (elephant-thighs); nāgarājā + nāgapāso lean serpent; nāganāsūrū=elephant token incidental. Dominant nāgarājā = serpent
nāgadānena khiyanti 'they dwell by gift to nāgas'? glossed khiyati=nivasati; obscure but nāgadāna = offering to nāgas implies serpent-beings; default serpent

claim: no marriage was ever made with nāgas before — how can we wed nāgas? (na no vivāho nāgehi katapubbo)
note: Jataka verse on nāga intermarriage; asserts nāga-human relations/separateness; serpent
nāgāvalokitaṃ 'the elephant-look' (turning the whole body to look back, the Buddha's gesture named after the elephant's manner of looking); nāga = elephant
āyasmā Nāgaseno, bhante Nāgasena (Milindapañha); the monk Nāgasena; nāgaṃ/nāgo incidental grammar
nāganāsūrū 'elephant-trunk thighs' in ū-stem feminine paradigm (vāmūrū nāganāsūrū); nāgī form; trunk = elephant

punnāga in tree/rukkha list (punnāga pūga panasa... karañja rukkhā); nāga in a beings-list also appears (naro-raga nāga yakkhā) but the live distinctive token punnāga = tree. Mixed beings+trees; coding tree for punnāga, the operative new lexeme; note also nāga(being) in surā-asura list
namo karohi nāgassa 'pay homage to the nāga'; dative-grammar example, referent not fixed
nāgamāṇavikā 'nāga-maiden' (she-nāga in human guise, kammadhāraya example); buddhanāgo (epithet) and punnāgo (tree) also present; mixed; live distinctive nāgamāṇavikā = serpent-maiden
nāgagati/nāgavikkamo 'elephant's gait/stride' similes for the Buddha's marks; nāgassa viya gati
nāgaro/nāgarako/nāgariko/nāgaratā 'town-born, urbane, citizen' (nagare jāto = nāgaro); taddhita derivation of nāgara
kānubandhanāgamesū = (n)āgama grammatical augment rule
nāggahite = na+gahite 'when not taken'; sandhi false friend (na+aggahite)
nāgo ca supaṇṇo ca 'a nāga and a garuḷa' in natural-enemy dvanda list (ahi-nakula, kāka-ulūka); nāga vs supaṇṇa = serpent
kānubandhanāgamesū = (n)āgama grammatical augment rule
nāgame/tampināgame = (asati) nāgame 'when there is no augment'; grammatical augment
punnāga in tree/rukkha list (punnāga pūga panasa... karañja rukkhā); nāga(being) also in surā-asura-naro-raga-nāga list; live distinctive token punnāga = tree
nāgī (she-nāga) in feminine-declension list gandhabbī kinnarī nāgī devī yakkhī; among classes of female beings = serpent
nāgamo/nāgamopi = (n)āgama grammatical augment ('āgamo vibhattissa ādyāvayavo')
assa rañño nāgaṃ dhārayāma 'we owe/keep the king's elephant'; royal-elephant debt grammar example

buddhanāgo in upamā list munisīho/munivasabho/munipuṅgavo/buddhanāgo/buddhādicco; nāga as honorific of the Buddha; nāgamāṇavikā (serpent-maiden) incidental example. Dominant operative = buddhanāgo epithet
nāgagati/nāgavikkamo 'elephant's gait/stride' similes; nāgassa gati viya
nāginī nāgī in feminine-suffix rule yakkhinī/nāginī/sīhinī; she-nāga form; default serpent (paired with yakkhī)
nāgamesū = (n)āgamesu grammatical augment rule (na te kānubandhanāgamesū)
nāgame/nāgamesū = (n)āgama grammatical augment
nāgamassa = (n)āgamassa 'of the augment'; ūāgame nāgamassa ṇe; grammar
nāgame = 'in the augment'; nāgame vinicchinitabbaṃ; grammatical augment
nāgamo = (n)āgamo grammatical augment; ūmhi nāgamo
kānubandhanāgamesū = (n)āgama grammatical augment rule
kānubandhanāgamesū = (n)āgama grammatical augment rule
nāgame = 'in the augment'; kuāgame nāgame ca; grammatical augment
nāgame/nāgamattā = (n)āgama grammatical augment ('nāgamattā na vuddhi')

claim: Virūpakkha is named lord of the nāgas (nāgādhipati) among the four world-guardians
note: Abhidhānappadīpikā cosmology: viruḷhako, virūpakkho tu nāgādhipatīrito; nāgas governed by Virūpakkha
Abhidhānappadīpikā synonym verse: kuñjaro vāraṇo hatthī mātaṅgo dvirado gajo, nāgo dvipo ibho dantī; nāga = elephant; dhanāgamo=dhana+āgamo (nonlexical) incidental
